title: zzcollab CLI Changelog
text: Version history zzcollab bash CLI template framework (2.x line). R package release notes NEWS.md.

title: zzcollab CLI Changelog
text: Removed renv Imports. renv activated .Rprofile never called package code, declaring hard dependency made R CMD check fail Package required available: 'renv' (renv::restore() install renv project library). removal applied fallback generator modules/project.sh.

title: zzcollab CLI Changelog
text: Added _R_CHECK_FORCE_SUGGESTS_: 'false' job environment suggested package check library provide (e.g. tinytest) treated error.

title: zzcollab CLI Changelog
text: Fixed four defects. hash-data/verify-data read RAW_DATA_DIR variable defaulting analysis/data/raw_data (previously scanned non-existent top-level raw_data/). check/docker-check depend build/docker-build-pkg, tarball exists R CMD check. team targets guard unset DOCKERHUB_ACCOUNT. duplicate docker-check-renv/docker-check-renv-fix delegate check-renv--fix/check-renv rather duplicating commands. Factored repeated docker run single DOCKER_RUN variable. pins --platform uniformly across batch targets (previously ran host architecture cached test target CI ran amd64) derives mount work path Dockerfile’s ARG USERNAME (default analyst) instead hardcoding /home/analyst/project. Best-practice cleanup. Self-documenting help generated ## target annotations ##@ section markers; docker-clean removes project’s image (system-wide prune left docker-prune-/-cache); docker-prune-* use $(MAKE); .PHONY adds docker-script rstudio drops dead ; fail-fast recipes via SHELL=/bin/bash .SHELLFLAGS=-eu -c.

title: zzcollab CLI Changelog
text: ZZCOLLAB_TEMPLATE_VERSION bumped 2.8.0 2.8.1.

title: zzcollab CLI Changelog
text: Sys.glob('renv/library/*/*')[1] now wrapped normalizePath(..., mustWork = FALSE) added .libPaths(). bare glob returns relative path; rcmdcheck::rcmdcheck() spawns R CMD check temp directory, relative library paths resolve nothing packages installed renv::restore() (e.g. markdown, rmarkdown) invisible vignette-building subprocess. absolute path survives working-directory change. Rscript ---init-file added Run R CMD check step, consistent deployed majority explicit .libPaths() setup follows.

title: zzcollab CLI Changelog
text: ZZCOLLAB_TEMPLATE_VERSION bumped 2.5.0 2.8.0 exceed v2.7.0 stamp previously deployed 35 render-report.yml files. 2.5.0, doctor reported deployed files forward-newer template never offered replacement; 2.8.0, doctor correctly identifies workflow files outdated offers interactive replacement.

title: zzcollab CLI Changelog
text: CI now renders manuscripts HTML via output_format = "html_document" rather PDF. HTML output validates R code execution package reproducibility without requiring LaTeX installation inside Docker container. PDF rendering remains host using local xelatex toolchain. Removed Install TinyTeX host runner Make TinyTeX tree accessible writable container steps entirely. Removed -v $HOME/.TinyTeX:/opt/tinytex -e PATH=... bind mounts docker run invocation. Renamed artifact rendered-manuscripts / *.pdf rendered-html / *.html. output_format argument uses R double-quotes ("html_document") rather single quotes, required R code wrapped Rscript -e '...' single-quoted shell argument. Version stamp added template header zzc doctor can track propagate future updates.

title: zzcollab CLI Changelog
text: Added version-stamp tracking .github/workflows/render-report.yml. zzc doctor now reports render-report.yml version alongside r-package.yml, Makefile, .Rprofile, Dockerfile. Outdated missing render-report.yml triggers [y/N] replacement prompt (behaviour r-package.yml), full template body replaced rather just stamp line. Help text updated list render-report.yml version-stamps section.

title: zzcollab CLI Changelog
text: trivy-scan job marked continue--error: true intermittent Docker Hub pull timeouts block CI jobs. Docker build step gains 3-attempt retry loop 30-second backoff attempts. Step-level timeout-minutes: 15 added bound worst-case wall time.

title: zzcollab Changelog

Older hand-trimmed configs lack whole sections (author, license, r_package, style, github) gain sections default values comments, every value already set preserved. title: zzcollab Changelog

title: zzcollab Changelog

title: zzcollab Changelog
text: Re-baseline. CLI, template stamp, R package version drifted apart (2.8.1, 2.0.0, 0.9.2 respectively); unified single ZZCOLLAB_VERSION source truth lib/constants.sh reset 0.1.0 mark new start. earlier 2.x history preserved CHANGELOG-2.x.md. release establishes presence-driven reproducibility-toggle architecture (see docs/reproducibility-toggle-plan-whitepaper.md).

title: zzcollab Changelog

title: Contributing to zzcollab
text: Thank interest contributing. zzcollab combines bash CLI (zzcollab.sh modules/) R package (R/, man/, vignettes/). must stay step.

title: Contributing to zzcollab
text: opening issue, please search existing issues https://github.com/rgt47/zzcollab/issues confirm problem already reported. opening new issue, include: minimal reproducible example. CLI bugs, exact command surrounding shell context. R bugs, reprex::reprex(). output zzcollab --version bash --version (CLI side) sessionInfo() (R side). Docker profile use, applicable. expected behaviour observed behaviour.

title: Contributing to zzcollab
text: non-trivial changes, please open issue first discuss approach. zzcollab well-defined extension points (Docker profiles bundles.yaml, configuration layers, auto-snapshot pipeline); brief design discussion saves rework.

title: Contributing to zzcollab
text: Fork repository create topic branch main. Install development dependencies. zzcollab uses renv R package side: shell development, ensure shellcheck, bats-core, jq, curl installed. Make changes. Keep commits focused; prefer many small commits one large one. Update tests: R changes: tests/testthat/ CLI changes: tests/integration/ (bats) tests/shellcheck/ Run relevant local checks: Update NEWS.md (R package side) CHANGELOG.md (CLI/framework side) depending surface changed. Use affected. Open pull request main. Reference related issues.
code: renv::restore() devtools::document() devtools::check() make test make check-renv shellcheck zzcollab.sh modules/*.sh

title: Contributing to zzcollab
text: Use native R pipe (|>); avoid %>% new code. Use <- assignment, never =. Use snake_case functions variables. Prefer implicit returns; reserve return() early exits. Document exported functions roxygen2. must @title, @description, @param, @return, @examples. Two-space indentation. Single quotes character literals.

title: Contributing to zzcollab
text: POSIX-compatible feasible; bash features needed (declare #!/usr/bin/env bash). shellcheck must pass. CI runs shellcheck.yml workflow. Two-space indentation, snake_case variables. Quote variable expansions: "${var}", never $var. Modules modules/ follow existing core.sh / cli.sh pattern: function documented top purpose, inputs, outputs.

title: Contributing to zzcollab
text: NEWS.md tracks R package version line (currently 0.9.x). follows tidyverse style (# zzcollab X.Y.Z headers + bullet lists) R CMD check reads. CHANGELOG.md tracks CLI/framework version line (currently 2.x). pre-dates R package uses date-headed sections. change touches surfaces, add bullet . touches one, edit file.

title: Contributing to zzcollab
text: participating project, agree abide Code Conduct.

Stale profile names (bioinformatics, geospatial, publishing) removed configuration roxygen examples; profiles now minimal, analysis, rstudio.

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: ZZCOLLAB? Quick Start Docker Profile System Project Structure Development Workflow Package Management Team Collaboration Build System Make Configuration System GitHub Actions CI/CD GitLab support Troubleshooting Platform-Specific Notes

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: ZZCOLLAB framework creating reproducible research compendia. compendium self-contained project combines code, data, documentation, computational environment specification enable complete reproducibility.

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: Docker-based reproducibility isolated computational environments Two-layer architecture separating team infrastructure individual package management Three Docker profiles ranging ~650 MB ~1.2 GB Pure-shell dependency validation (host R required) Automatic renv snapshot container exit Content-addressable Docker build caching GitHub Actions CI/CD testing validation

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: ZZCOLLAB uses two-layer reproducibility model: Layer 1 – Docker Profile (team/shared). team lead selects Docker profile defines base image, system libraries, pre-installed R packages. layer fixed chosen shared team members via Docker Hub. Layer 2 – renv.lock (personal/collaborative). team member can add R packages inside container using standard install.packages() calls. .Rprofile auto-snapshots renv.lock exit, lock file accumulates packages contributors. five pillars zzcollab workspace : Dockerfile + renv.lock + .Rprofile + source code + data.

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: Docker installed running Git version control Make build automation GitHub CLI (gh) repository management (optional)

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
code: mkdir my-analysis && cd my-analysis zzc analysis                    # full setup with tidyverse make docker-build && make r     # build image, start R

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
code: zzc config set dockerhub-account mylab   # one-time config mkdir study && cd study zzc analysis                    # full setup zzc dockerhub                   # push image to Docker Hub zzc github                      # create GitHub repo and push

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
code: git clone https://github.com/mylab/study.git cd study make docker-build               # build from Dockerfile make r                          # start development

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
code: make r                          # enter container # ... work in R ... q()                             # exit (auto-snapshots renv.lock) make docker-test                # run tests git add . && git commit -m "Add analysis" && git push

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: profile predefined combination base image, system libraries, R packages. Select one zzc <profile>: specialized environments, build base image instead profile (see Custom Composition): workspace already exists, running zzc <profile> switches Docker profile regenerating Dockerfile.
code: zzc analysis                    # new project with tidyverse zzc docker --base-image rocker/verse   # LaTeX + Quarto environment zzc list profiles               # show all profiles with descriptions

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: Build specific base image built-profile fits: Additional R packages added via renv inside container (see Package Management). built-profile bundles curated package set; bundles document sets: R Package Bundles (built profiles):
code: zzc docker --base-image rocker/r-ver zzc list pkgs                   # show all package bundles zzc list libs                   # show all system library bundles zzc list                        # show everything

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: ZZCOLLAB scans code R package usage maps packages required system libraries: eliminates manual library specification workflows.
code: make check-system-deps          # report missing system deps

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: Running zzc analysis empty directory creates following:
code: myproject/ ├── DESCRIPTION                 # R package metadata ├── NAMESPACE                   # Export list (roxygen2-generated) ├── LICENSE                     # Copyright file ├── Makefile                    # Build automation ├── Dockerfile                  # Container definition ├── renv.lock                   # Package dependency lock file ├── .Rprofile                   # renv activation + auto-snapshot ├── .gitignore                  # Git exclusions ├── .Rbuildignore               # R CMD build exclusions ├── R/                          # Reusable R functions ├── man/                        # Function documentation ├── tests/ │   └── tinytest.R              # Test runner ├── inst/ │   └── tinytest/ │       └── test-basic.R        # Example test ├── analysis/ │   ├── data/ │   │   ├── raw_data/           # Original untransformed data │   │   └── derived_data/       # Processed data │   ├── report/                 # Analysis reports │   ├── figures/                # Generated plots │   ├── tables/                 # Generated tables │   └── scripts/                # Analysis scripts ├── docs/ │   └── ZZCOLLAB_USER_GUIDE.md  # This guide ├── vignettes/                  # Package vignettes └── .github/     └── workflows/              # GitHub Actions CI/CD

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: Instead using profile shortcut, can build workspace incrementally:
code: zzc init                        # R package structure only zzc renv                        # add renv (renv.lock, .Rprofile) zzc docker                      # add Docker (Dockerfile) zzc git                         # initialize git repository zzc github                      # create GitHub repo and push

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: target: Runs zzcollab validate --fix --strict --verbose check package dependencies starting. Starts Docker container project directory renv cache mounted. Launches R inside container.
code: make r

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
code: devtools::load_all()            # load package functions devtools::test()                # run tests install.packages("newpkg")      # add a package

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: exit, two things happen automatically: Auto-snapshot (inside container): .Rprofile .Last() hook runs renv::snapshot(prompt = FALSE) update renv.lock. Post-session validation (host): Makefile runs zzcollab validate --fix --strict --verbose verify DESCRIPTION renv.lock consistent code usage.

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: Opens RStudio http://localhost:8787 (username: rstudio, password: rstudio).
code: make rstudio

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: adding packages, rebuild Docker image includes :
code: make docker-build               # content-addressable: skips if unchanged make docker-rebuild             # force full rebuild (no cache)

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: Layer 1 (Docker image): Pre-installed packages selected profile. compile image build time shared team members. Controlled team lead’s profile choice. Layer 2 (renv.lock): Dynamic packages added team member inside container. lock file source truth reproducibility, Docker image.

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: GitHub packages:
code: make r install.packages("survival") q()                             # auto-snapshot writes renv.lock renv::install("user/package")

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: Controlled environment variable ZZCOLLAB_AUTO_SNAPSHOT (default: true). R exits inside container, .Last() function .Rprofile runs renv::snapshot(prompt = FALSE).

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: Controlled ZZCOLLAB_AUTO_RESTORE (default: true). R starts inside container, .Rprofile runs renv::restore(prompt = FALSE) install missing packages renv.lock.

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: Controlled ZZCOLLAB_AUTO_INIT (default: true). renv.lock exists DESCRIPTION file present, .Rprofile initializes renv automatically.

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide

title: ZZCOLLAB User Guide
text: zzcollab supports GitLab alternative GitHub. active forge single choice, set : forge can also chosen interactively zzc toggle (forge picker), first decision feature wizard. forge=gitlab, framework adapts follows: CI: single .gitlab-ci.yml replaces two GitHub Actions workflows. self-adapts backend (Nix flake, Docker, renv, DESCRIPTION) runs check stage path-filtered render stage, mirroring GitHub pipeline. Install remove zzc add cicd / zzc rm cicd, via ci feature zzc toggle. Remote repository: zzc gitlab (counterpart zzc github) uses glab CLI create project push, honouring gitlab-host self-hosted instances. Visibility --private (default), --public, --internal. Container registry: pushes default GitLab Container Registry (registry.gitlab.com, registry.<host> self-hosted), whose CI authentication provided automatically GitLab’s $CI_REGISTRY variables. Override zzc config set docker-registry <host>. Push zzc push (registry-aware command; zzc dockerhub back-compat alias). Cloud launch: zzc cloud installs GitLab Workspaces devfile (.devfile.yaml) instead Codespaces devcontainer. Force either zzc cloud --platform workspace --platform devcontainer. GitLab integration requires glab CLI repository creation (zzc gitlab); CI, registry, Workspaces configuration generated without .
code: zzc config set forge gitlab zzc config set gitlab-account mylab zzc config set gitlab-host gitlab.example.edu   # self-hosted only

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: ZZCOLLAB’s configuration system provides flexible control Docker environments, package management, team collaboration multi-layered configuration hierarchy comprehensive CLI flags. Key terms vignette. New vocabulary? terms guide uses; defined first use. Full definitions vignette('glossary'). Profile: Docker image bundle environment built (minimal, analysis, rstudio). Two-layer package management: shared, image-baked packages (Layer 1) versus per-user packages added runtime (Layer 2). Package backend (renv / Nix / none): project records exact package versions; single mutually-exclusive choice. Auto-snapshot / auto-restore: zzcollab records installed packages renv.lock exit installs missing ones start. Base image: starting Docker image project builds (e.g. rocker/tidyverse, rocker/verse). Team image: pre-built Docker image shared via registry every collaborator works byte-identical environment. zzc / zzcollab: framework’s command-line interface; zzc alias zzcollab.

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: Multi-layered configuration: User, project, system, built-defaults Docker profile system: three built-profiles (minimal, analysis, rstudio), environments available via custom base image Dynamic package management: Add packages via install.packages() automatic snapshot/restore Two-layer architecture: Docker profiles (team/shared) + renv packages (personal/flexible) CLI R interfaces: Work via command line entirely within R/RStudio

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: ZZCOLLAB follows “configure , use everywhere” approach: - Set defaults configuration files - Override per-project needed - Eliminate repetitive flag typing - Share team configurations via version control

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: ZZCOLLAB loads configuration multiple sources: Command-line flags - Explicit flags override settings Environment variables - Shell environment variables (e.g., ZZCOLLAB_TEAM_NAME) Project config - ./zzcollab.yaml current directory (team-specific) User config - ~/.zzcollab/config.yaml (personal defaults) System config - /etc/zzcollab/config.yaml (organization-wide) Built-defaults - Fallback values ensuring functionality Higher priority sources override lower priority ones, allowing precise control level.

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: Personal defaults across projects: use: Set personal preferences , apply future projects.
code: # ~/.zzcollab/config.yaml defaults:   team-name: "myteam"   github-account: "myusername"   dockerhub-account: "myusername"   profile-name: "analysis"  # Optional: Custom profile compositions profiles:   my-custom:     base-image: "rocker/r-ver:latest"     libs-bundle: "minimal"     pkgs-bundle: "tidyverse"

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: Team-specific settings shared via Git: use: Share team standards, ensure members use identical configurations.
code: # ./zzcollab.yaml (in project root) team:   name: "genomicslab"   project: "cancer-study"   description: "Multi-omics cancer research"  defaults:   profile-name: "analysis"  collaboration:   github:     auto-create-repo: true     default-visibility: "private"     enable-actions: true

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: Organization-wide policies (requires admin): use: administrators setting organization-wide policies.
code: # /etc/zzcollab/config.yaml organization:   name: "University Research Computing"   docker-registry: "registry.university.edu"  defaults:   profile-name: "analysis"  collaboration:   github:     default-visibility: "private"  # Enforce private repos     enable-actions: true

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: ZZCOLLAB uses two-layer system maximum flexibility reproducibility.

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: : Pre-installed R packages Docker image Control: profile (zzc <profile> / docker --profile) custom base image Shared: team members get identical packages Purpose: Fast container startup, team consistency Layer 1 packages Docker image: - Available immediately container starts - installation time - Shared across users image - Fixed image rebuilt
code: # Team lead creates base environment mkdir study && cd study zzc analysis                     # ML packages added via renv on top of analysis zzc github make docker-build zzc dockerhub                    # Push for team members  # All team members get identical environment

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: : Packages added via install.packages() inside containers automatic snapshot exit Control: Team members install needed Flexible: person can add different packages Purpose: Analysis-specific packages, reproducible dependency tracking Layer 2 packages via renv: - Installed -demand inside containers - Added dynamically analysis needs evolve - Accumulates packages team members - renv.lock source truth reproducibility
code: # Inside container make r  # Install packages as needed (standard R command) install.packages("tidymodels") install.packages("pins") install.packages("vetiver")  # When you exit R, packages are automatically saved to renv.lock (via .Last() in .Rprofile) q()  # Back on host - commit the updated renv.lock git add renv.lock DESCRIPTION git commit -m "Add ML deployment packages" git push

previous_headings: Two-Layer Package Management Architecture > How the Layers Work Together
what: Scenario: Genomics Team with Multiple Analyses
title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: Team Lead Setup (Layer 1): Team Member Alice (Layer 2 - RNA-seq): Team Member Bob (Layer 2 - ChIP-seq): Result: - use identical Layer 1 (Bioconductor base via team image) - different Layer 2 (analysis-specific packages renv.lock) - Complete reproducibility via renv.lock (source truth)
code: # Create a base environment with common packages mkdir baseenv && cd baseenv zzc analysis                     # Or another profile zzc github make docker-build zzc dockerhub                    # Push for team members git clone https://github.com/genomicslab/rnaseq-study.git cd rnaseq-study make docker-build                # Or: docker pull genomicslab/rnaseq-study:latest make r  # Add RNA-seq specific packages install.packages(c("tximport", "DESeq2", "apeglm", "EnhancedVolcano")) # Auto-snapshot on exit captures these packages in renv.lock q()  # Exit R - snapshot happens automatically git clone https://github.com/genomicslab/chipseq-study.git cd chipseq-study make docker-build make r  # Add ChIP-seq specific packages install.packages(c("ChIPseeker", "ChIPpeakAnno", "DiffBind", "csaw")) # Auto-snapshot on exit captures these packages in renv.lock q()  # Exit R - snapshot happens automatically

what: Key Principle: renv.lock is Source of Truth
title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: Docker images provide foundation performance, renv.lock ensures reproducibility: works: renv.lock specifies exact package versions CRAN/Bioconductor/GitHub snapshots, independent Docker image.
code: # Five years later, reproduce Alice's RNA-seq analysis git clone https://github.com/genomicslab/rnaseq-study.git cd rnaseq-study  # Can use ANY compatible Docker base image zzcollab --profile minimal  # or analysis, or rstudio make r  # renv automatically restores EXACT packages Alice used on R startup # Auto-restore installs: tximport, DESeq2, apeglm, EnhancedVolcano at exact versions # (No manual renv::restore() call needed - happens automatically)  # Run analysis - identical results Rscript analysis/scripts/differential_expression.R

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: Goal: Multiple specialized environments, organization-wide standards System Admin Setup (/etc/zzcollab/config.yaml): Core Director Setup: Researcher Workflow:
code: organization:   name: "Bioinformatics Core"   docker-registry: "registry.core.university.edu"  defaults:   github-account: "bioinformatics-core"  collaboration:   github:     default-visibility: "private"     enable-actions: true # Base images for different workflows mkdir biocore-rnaseq && cd biocore-rnaseq zzc analysis                     # Full setup; add genomics packages via renv zzc github make docker-build && make docker-push-team  cd .. && mkdir biocore-chipseq && cd biocore-chipseq zzc analysis zzc github make docker-build && make docker-push-team  cd .. && mkdir biocore-singlecell && cd biocore-singlecell zzc analysis zzc github make docker-build && make docker-push-team # Join workflow-specific project git clone https://github.com/biocore/rnaseq-study.git cd rnaseq-study make docker-build  # Build from project Dockerfile  # Add analysis-specific packages make r install.packages("workflow_specific_package") # Auto-snapshot on exit captures the package q()  # Exit R - snapshot happens automatically

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: Goal: Client projects, quarterly environment updates, reproducible deliverables DevOps Team Setup: Data Scientist Workflow:
code: # Quarterly standardized environment mkdir q1-2025-base && cd q1-2025-base zzc analysis                     # Full setup; ML packages added via renv zzc github  # Document package versions cat > ENVIRONMENT.md << 'EOF' # Q1 2025 Standardized Environment  Base: rocker/ml:latest (R 4.4.0) Profile: analysis  Key packages: - tidyverse 2.0.0 - tidymodels 1.2.0 - xgboost 1.7.6 - shiny 1.8.0  Last updated: 2025-01-15 EOF  make docker-build make docker-push-team git add . && git commit -m "Q1 2025 base environment" && git push # Create client project using quarterly base mkdir client-churn-prediction && cd client-churn-prediction zzc analysis                     # Full setup; ML packages added via renv zzc github  # Team base image provides foundation make r  # Add client-specific packages install.packages(c("probably", "workflowsets", "themis")) # Auto-snapshot on exit captures these packages q()  # Exit R - snapshot happens automatically  # All projects use same Q1 2025 foundation # Client-specific packages in renv.lock

code: # Symptom: Set profile-name in user config but project uses different profile zzcollab config set profile-name "analysis" zzcollab config get profile-name # Output: rstudio  (Expected: analysis) zzcollab config list --verbose # Output shows: # profile-name: "rstudio" #   Source: ./zzcollab.yaml (project config) [OVERRIDES user setting]  # Project config (./zzcollab.yaml) overrides user config # Remove from project config or understand this is intentional vim ./zzcollab.yaml  # Remove profile-name to use user default

previous_headings: Configuration Validation and Troubleshooting > Common Issues and Solutions
title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: Problem: ARM64 Mac build AMD64-base image. Solution 1: Use ARM64-compatible base add packages via renv Solution 2: Force AMD64 platform (runs emulation)
code: # Error when building on the rocker/geospatial base image: # Error: rocker/geospatial:latest: no matching manifest for linux/arm64/v8 zzcollab analysis # Then add spatial packages inside the container: # install.packages(c("sf", "terra", "raster")) # (Auto-snapshot on exit saves these to renv.lock) export DOCKER_DEFAULT_PLATFORM=linux/amd64 zzcollab docker --base-image rocker/geospatial # Slower under emulation, but compatible

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
code: library(zzcollab)  # Initialize configuration init_config()  # Set configuration values set_config("team_name", "myteam") set_config("profile_name", "analysis") set_config("github_account", "myusername")  # Get configuration values team <- get_config("team_name") profile <- get_config("profile_name")  # List all configuration all_config <- list_config() print(all_config)  # Validate configuration valid <- validate_config() if (!valid$valid) {   message("Configuration errors:")   print(valid$errors) }

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: ZZCOLLAB’s configuration system provides control reproducible research environments multiple layers:

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: Configuration hierarchy: Command-line → environment → project → user → system → defaults Docker profiles: three built-profiles (minimal ~650MB, analysis ~1.2GB, rstudio ~980MB); environments via custom base image Two-layer architecture: Docker profiles (shared) + renv packages (flexible) CLI flags: Comprehensive control aspects R interface: Complete configuration management within R

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: Initialize configuration: Create project: Team collaboration: Package management:
code: zzcollab config init zzcollab config set team-name "myteam" zzcollab config set profile-name "analysis" mkdir study && cd study zzc analysis                     # Full setup (init + renv + docker) zzc github                       # Create private GitHub repo # Team lead: make docker-build && make docker-push-team  # Team member: git clone https://github.com/myteam/study.git && cd study make docker-build # Inside container: install.packages("package") # Auto-snapshot on exit - no manual snapshot needed q()  # Exit R - snapshot happens automatically

title: ZZCOLLAB Configuration System
text: zzcollab --help - Complete CLI reference help(package = "zzcollab") - R package documentation vignette("getting-started") - Comprehensive tutorial docs/VARIANTS.md - three built-profiles custom base-image workflow specialized environments docs/CONFIGURATION.md - Advanced configuration guide

title: Getting Started with ZZCOLLAB
text: tutorial provides systematic approach establishing reproducible research workflow using ZZCOLLAB framework. Palmer Penguins dataset (Gorman et al., 2014) serves illustrative example implementing computational reproducibility practices data analysis. Key terms vignette. New vocabulary? terms guide uses; defined first use. Full definitions vignette('glossary'). Computational reproducibility: getting numerical results data code. Docker / container / image / Dockerfile: tool packages software environment; running instance, template, recipe builds . Profile: Docker image bundle environment built (minimal, analysis, rstudio). Two-layer package management: shared, image-baked packages (Layer 1) versus per-user packages added runtime (Layer 2). renv: R package giving project isolated, version-pinned package library. Raw vs derived data: immutable original data files versus processed, analysis-ready datasets generated . Unit test / testthat / tinytest: focused check one piece code behaves intended. Data provenance / lineage: record data came transformed.

title: Getting Started with ZZCOLLAB
text: Upon completion tutorial, researchers able : Initialize containerized research project appropriate dependency management Implement proper data organization separating immutable raw data derived datasets Develop comprehensive validation tests data integrity Navigate host container environments development workflows Integrate personal configuration files containerized environments Document data transformations following research reproducibility standards

previous_headings: Docker Profile Selection > Reproducibility-Critical Environment Variables
title: Getting Started with ZZCOLLAB
text: Locale Settings (LANG, LC_ALL): String sorting: “Åland” sorts differently US vs Swedish locales Number parsing: European format “3,14159” becomes NA US locale Factor ordering: Affects reference categories statistical models Without explicit locale settings, two researchers running identical code different systems can produce different statistical results. Timezone (TZ): Date arithmetic: Daylight saving time causes missing/duplicate timestamps Time aggregation: Different timezones produce different daily summaries Temporal analyses: Ensures consistent time-based calculations Parallel Processing (OMP_NUM_THREADS): Deterministic execution: Single-threaded execution (=1) ensures identical results Floating-point order: Multi-threaded summation can vary due operation order Random number generation: Prevents race conditions stochastic analyses

title: Getting Started with ZZCOLLAB
text: Critical Reproducibility: Data documentation essential code documentation. Without comprehensive data provenance metadata, even perfect computational reproducibility enable independent verification research findings. Complete documentation data sources enables verification citation: Add following metadata Data Sources section:
code: vim analysis/data/README.md ## Data Sources and Provenance  ### penguins_raw.csv  - **Source**: Palmer Archipelago (Antarctica) Long Term Ecological   Research (LTER) Program - **Collection Period**: 2007-2009 - **Acquisition Date**: 2025-10-02 - **Original Location**: palmerpenguins R package (version 0.1.1) - **DOI**: https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pone.0090081 - **Citation**: Gorman KB, Williams TD, Fraser WR (2014) Ecological   Sexual Dimorphism and Environmental Variability within a Community   of Antarctic Penguins (Genus Pygoscelis). PLoS ONE 9(3): e90081. - **File Size**: 14 KB - **MD5 Checksum**: [compute via md5sum analysis/data/raw_data/penguins_raw.csv] - **Observations**: 344 individuals - **Variables**: 8 measurements

title: Getting Started with ZZCOLLAB
text: Comprehensive variable documentation facilitates proper interpretation:
code: ## Data Dictionary  ### penguins_raw.csv  | Variable          | Type    | Description                   | |-------------------|---------|-------------------------------| | species           | Factor  | Penguin species               | |                   |         | (Adelie, Chinstrap, Gentoo)   | | island            | Factor  | Island location               | |                   |         | (Biscoe, Dream, Torgersen)    | | bill_length_mm    | Numeric | Culmen length                 | | bill_depth_mm     | Numeric | Culmen depth                  | | flipper_length_mm | Integer | Flipper length                | | body_mass_g       | Integer | Body mass                     | | sex               | Factor  | Individual sex                | |                   |         | (male, female)                | | year              | Integer | Study year                    |  | Variable          | Units | Valid Range | Missing Codes | |-------------------|-------|-------------|---------------| | species           | -     | 3 levels    | NA            | | island            | -     | 3 levels    | NA            | | bill_length_mm    | mm    | 30-60       | NA            | | bill_depth_mm     | mm    | 13-22       | NA            | | flipper_length_mm | mm    | 170-235     | NA            | | body_mass_g       | g     | 2700-6300   | NA            | | sex               | -     | 2 levels    | NA            | | year              | year  | 2007-2009   | NA            |  ### Missing Data Summary  - bill_length_mm: 2 missing (0.6%) - bill_depth_mm: 2 missing (0.6%) - flipper_length_mm: 2 missing (0.6%) - body_mass_g: 2 missing (0.6%) - sex: 11 missing (3.2%) - Total observations: 344 penguins

title: Getting Started with ZZCOLLAB
text: Automated testing ensures raw data integrity detects unauthorized modifications immutable source files.

title: Getting Started with ZZCOLLAB
text: data workflow documentation: Data validation testing prevents silent errors compromise research integrity. Tests serve multiple functions: detecting accidental modifications, validating quality assumptions, documenting expected properties, enabling reproducible research workflows.

title: Getting Started with ZZCOLLAB
text: Create comprehensive validation tests: Implement following test suite. ZZCOLLAB projects use tinytest, tests bare top-level expectations test_that() wrapper:
code: # Execute from host environment vim inst/tinytest/test-raw-data-validation.R # inst/tinytest/test-raw-data-validation.R  #======================================================================= # RAW DATA VALIDATION TESTS #======================================================================= # Purpose: Ensure raw data maintains unchanging characteristics # Rationale: Raw data immutability requires validation against #            accidental modifications  data_file <- 'analysis/data/raw_data/penguins_raw.csv'  # Verify file existence expect_true(file.exists(data_file))  # Verify file readability and capture for subsequent checks data <- read.csv(data_file)  # Validate observation count (raw data invariant) expect_equal(nrow(data), 344L)  # Validate variable count expect_equal(ncol(data), 8L)  # Validate column names and ordering expected_cols <- c(   'species', 'island', 'bill_length_mm', 'bill_depth_mm',   'flipper_length_mm', 'body_mass_g', 'sex', 'year' ) expect_equal(names(data), expected_cols)  # Re-read with factor conversion for type and level checks data_f <- read.csv(data_file, stringsAsFactors = TRUE)  # Validate categorical variables expect_true(is.factor(data_f$species)) expect_true(is.factor(data_f$island)) expect_true(is.factor(data_f$sex))  # Validate numeric variables expect_true(is.numeric(data_f$bill_length_mm)) expect_true(is.numeric(data_f$bill_depth_mm))  # Validate integer variables expect_true(is.integer(data_f$flipper_length_mm)) expect_true(is.integer(data_f$body_mass_g)) expect_true(is.integer(data_f$year))  # Validate factor levels (alphabetical order) expect_equal(levels(data_f$species),              c('Adelie', 'Chinstrap', 'Gentoo')) expect_equal(levels(data_f$island),              c('Biscoe', 'Dream', 'Torgersen')) expect_equal(levels(data_f$sex), c('female', 'male'))  # Validate values within expected ranges (drop NA first) bill_length <- data$bill_length_mm[!is.na(data$bill_length_mm)] bill_depth <- data$bill_depth_mm[!is.na(data$bill_depth_mm)] flipper <- data$flipper_length_mm[!is.na(data$flipper_length_mm)] body_mass <- data$body_mass_g[!is.na(data$body_mass_g)]  expect_true(all(bill_length >= 30 & bill_length <= 60)) expect_true(all(bill_depth >= 13 & bill_depth <= 22)) expect_true(all(flipper >= 170 & flipper <= 235)) expect_true(all(body_mass >= 2700 & body_mass <= 6300)) expect_true(all(data$year >= 2007 & data$year <= 2009))  # Validate missing-value pattern missing_counts <- colSums(is.na(data)) expect_equal(unname(missing_counts['bill_length_mm']), 2L) expect_equal(unname(missing_counts['bill_depth_mm']), 2L) expect_equal(unname(missing_counts['flipper_length_mm']), 2L) expect_equal(unname(missing_counts['body_mass_g']), 2L) expect_equal(unname(missing_counts['sex']), 11L) expect_equal(unname(missing_counts['species']), 0L) expect_equal(unname(missing_counts['island']), 0L) expect_equal(unname(missing_counts['year']), 0L)  # Validate sample sizes per group species_counts <- table(data_f$species) expect_equal(as.integer(species_counts['Adelie']), 152L) expect_equal(as.integer(species_counts['Chinstrap']), 68L) expect_equal(as.integer(species_counts['Gentoo']), 124L)  island_counts <- table(data_f$island) expect_equal(as.integer(island_counts['Biscoe']), 168L) expect_equal(as.integer(island_counts['Dream']), 124L) expect_equal(as.integer(island_counts['Torgersen']), 52L)  # File integrity: MD5 checksum format (32 hexadecimal characters) file_md5 <- tools::md5sum(data_file) expect_true(nchar(file_md5) == 32) # Document the checksum for future validation; in production, pin it: # expect_equal(unname(file_md5), 'known_hash_value')

title: Getting Started with ZZCOLLAB
text: Analysis-ready datasets created documented transformations raw data files.

title: Getting Started with ZZCOLLAB
text: Create data preparation function comprehensive documentation:
code: # Within container environment cat > R/prepare_penguin_data.R << 'EOF' #' Prepare Palmer Penguins Data for Analysis #' #' This function processes raw penguin morphometric data to create #' analysis-ready datasets through systematic transformations. #' #' Processing steps include: #' \itemize{ #'   \item Removal of observations with missing values #'   \item Addition of derived variables (ratios, transformations) #'   \item Proper ordering of factor levels for visualization #' } #' #' @param data_file Character string specifying path to raw data CSV #' @param n_records Integer specifying number of records to return #'   (NULL returns all complete cases) #' @return data.frame containing cleaned, analysis-ready data with #'   metadata attributes #' @export #' @examples #' penguins_clean <- prepare_penguin_data() #' summary(penguins_clean) prepare_penguin_data <- function(     data_file = "analysis/data/raw_data/penguins_raw.csv",     n_records = NULL) {    # Input validation   if (!file.exists(data_file)) {     stop("Data file not found: ", data_file)   }    # Load raw data with factor conversion   data <- read.csv(data_file, stringsAsFactors = TRUE)    # Remove incomplete cases   clean_data <- data[complete.cases(data), ]    # Generate derived variables for analysis   clean_data$bill_ratio <-     clean_data$bill_length_mm / clean_data$bill_depth_mm   clean_data$log_body_mass_g <- log(clean_data$body_mass_g)   clean_data$body_mass_kg <- clean_data$body_mass_g / 1000    # Order species factor by body mass for visualization   species_order <- c("Adelie", "Chinstrap", "Gentoo")   clean_data$species <-     factor(clean_data$species, levels = species_order)    # Subset to specified record count if requested   if (!is.null(n_records)) {     if (n_records > nrow(clean_data)) {       warning(         "Requested ", n_records, " records but only ",         nrow(clean_data), " available"       )     }     clean_data <-       clean_data[1:min(n_records, nrow(clean_data)), ]   }    # Attach metadata attributes   attr(clean_data, "preparation_date") <- Sys.Date()   attr(clean_data, "source_file") <- data_file   attr(clean_data, "n_removed") <- nrow(data) - nrow(clean_data)    return(clean_data) } EOF

title: Getting Started with ZZCOLLAB
text: Execute data preparation workflow within container:
code: # Start R session within container R  # Load project functions devtools::load_all()  # Execute preparation function penguins_clean <- prepare_penguin_data()  # Verify transformations summary(penguins_clean) str(penguins_clean)  # Inspect derived variables head(   penguins_clean[, c("species", "body_mass_g",                      "log_body_mass_g", "body_mass_kg",                      "bill_ratio")] )  # Verify record removal count attr(penguins_clean, "n_removed")  # Expected: 11 records  # Write to derived data directory saveRDS(penguins_clean, "analysis/data/derived_data/penguins_clean.rds") write.csv(   penguins_clean,   "analysis/data/derived_data/penguins_clean.csv",   row.names = FALSE )  # Exit R session quit(save = "no")

title: Getting Started with ZZCOLLAB
text: Document transformation process reproducibility: EOF
code: # Within container environment cat >> analysis/data/README.md << 'EOF'  ## Derived Data Files  ### penguins_clean.rds / penguins_clean.csv  **Description**: Analysis-ready Palmer Penguins dataset with complete cases and derived analytical variables.  **Source Script**: R/prepare_penguin_data.R **Source Data**: analysis/data/raw_data/penguins_raw.csv **Creation Date**: 2025-10-02  **Transformations Applied**:  1. Removed 11 observations with missing values (3.2% of dataset) 2. Calculated bill_ratio = bill_length_mm / bill_depth_mm 3. Calculated log_body_mass_g = log(body_mass_g) for log-linear    models 4. Calculated body_mass_kg = body_mass_g / 1000 for improved    interpretability 5. Ordered species factor by body mass    (Adelie < Chinstrap < Gentoo)  **Final Sample Size**: 333 complete observations  **Quality Assurance**:  - No missing values in any variable - All derived variables within expected ranges - Species distribution maintained: Adelie=146, Chinstrap=68,   Gentoo=119  **Usage Example**:  ```r # Load prepared data in R penguins_clean <- readRDS("analysis/data/derived_data/penguins_clean.rds") # Function Testing  Comprehensive unit tests ensure data preparation functions behave as specified across various input conditions.  ## Test Suite Development  Create tests for the preparation function:  ```bash # Within container environment cat > inst/tinytest/test-prepare-penguin-data.R << 'EOF' # tinytest expectations are bare top-level calls (no test_that wrapper). # devtools::load_all() (run by make docker-test) exposes package functions.  # Loads and cleans correctly penguins <- prepare_penguin_data() expect_inherits(penguins, 'data.frame') expect_false(any(is.na(penguins))) expect_equal(nrow(penguins), 333L) expect_true(all(c('bill_ratio', 'log_body_mass_g', 'body_mass_kg')                 %in% names(penguins)))  # Derived variables are computed correctly expect_true(all(penguins$bill_ratio > 0)) expect_equal(penguins$bill_ratio,              penguins$bill_length_mm / penguins$bill_depth_mm) expect_equal(penguins$log_body_mass_g, log(penguins$body_mass_g)) expect_equal(penguins$body_mass_kg, penguins$body_mass_g / 1000)  # Honours the n_records parameter penguins_50 <- prepare_penguin_data(n_records = 50) expect_equal(nrow(penguins_50), 50L) expect_warning(prepare_penguin_data(n_records = 500),                pattern = 'only.*available') penguins_many <- suppressWarnings(prepare_penguin_data(n_records = 500)) expect_equal(nrow(penguins_many), 333L)  # Adds metadata attributes expect_false(is.null(attr(penguins, 'preparation_date'))) expect_false(is.null(attr(penguins, 'source_file'))) expect_false(is.null(attr(penguins, 'n_removed'))) expect_equal(attr(penguins, 'n_removed'), 11L)  # Maintains species distribution species_counts <- table(penguins$species) expect_equal(length(species_counts), 3L) expect_true(species_counts['Adelie'] > 140) expect_true(species_counts['Gentoo'] > 110) expect_true(species_counts['Chinstrap'] > 60)  # Errors on an invalid file expect_error(prepare_penguin_data(data_file = 'nonexistent.csv'),              pattern = 'Data file not found') EOF

text: comprehensive analysis demonstrates complete workflow data preparation statistical modeling visualization.

title: Getting Started with ZZCOLLAB
text: Create complete analysis script host:
code: # Execute from host environment cat > scripts/01_penguin_bill_analysis.R << 'EOF' #======================================================================= # Palmer Penguins Bill Dimension Analysis #======================================================================= # Purpose: Examine relationship between bill dimensions and body mass #          across penguin species # Author: Research Team # Date: 2025-10-02  library(tidyverse) library(palmerpenguins) library(broom)  # Load prepared dataset penguins_clean <- readRDS("analysis/data/derived_data/penguins_clean.rds")  #======================================================================= # Exploratory Analysis #=======================================================================  # Calculate summary statistics by species summary_stats <- penguins_clean %>%   group_by(species) %>%   summarise(     n = n(),     mean_bill_length = mean(bill_length_mm),     sd_bill_length = sd(bill_length_mm),     mean_body_mass = mean(body_mass_g),     sd_body_mass = sd(body_mass_g),     .groups = "drop"   )  print("Summary Statistics by Species:") print(summary_stats)  #======================================================================= # Statistical Modeling #=======================================================================  # Fit linear model with interaction terms model <- lm(   body_mass_g ~ bill_length_mm * species +                 bill_depth_mm * species,   data = penguins_clean )  # Extract model coefficients and fit statistics model_summary <- tidy(model) model_fit <- glance(model)  print("\nModel Coefficients:") print(model_summary) print(paste("R-squared:", round(model_fit$r.squared, 3)))  #======================================================================= # Visualization #=======================================================================  # Create scatter plot with species-specific regression lines p1 <- ggplot(   penguins_clean,   aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = body_mass_g, color = species) ) +   geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +   geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE) +   labs(     title = "Penguin Body Mass vs Bill Length",     subtitle = "Palmer Archipelago Penguins (2007-2009)",     x = "Bill Length (mm)",     y = "Body Mass (g)",     color = "Species"   ) +   theme_minimal() +   theme(legend.position = "bottom")  # Write figure to file ggsave(   "analysis/figures/bill_length_vs_mass.png", p1,   width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300 )  print("\nAnalysis complete.") print("Figure: analysis/figures/bill_length_vs_mass.png")  #======================================================================= # Save Results #=======================================================================  # Compile results object results <- list(   summary_stats = summary_stats,   model = model,   model_summary = model_summary,   model_fit = model_fit,   plot = p1 )  # Write results to file saveRDS(results, "analysis/data/derived_data/bill_analysis_results.rds") print("Results: analysis/data/derived_data/bill_analysis_results.rds") EOF

title: Getting Started with ZZCOLLAB
text: ZZCOLLAB provides automated dependency checking ensure reproducibility. check implemented companion R package zzrenvcheck, installed project image. make check-renv runs zzrenvcheck::check_packages(auto_fix = TRUE, strict = TRUE) inside container; zzcollab validate wraps check host. default check-renv target strict auto-fixing: Verifies packages used R/ code declared DESCRIPTION Checks renv.lock matches actual package usage Scans tests/, vignettes/, inst/ addition R/ Validates packages across CRAN, Bioconductor, GitHub Repairs DESCRIPTION renv.lock discrepancies automatically Common validation scenarios: Interpreting validation results:
code: # Validate dependencies are synchronized (runs zzrenvcheck in the container) make check-renv # Standard validation (strict + auto-fix, recommended before every commit) make check-renv  # Report only, without modifying any files make check-renv-no-fix  # Skip the tests/ and vignettes/ scan make check-renv-no-strict # Success - ready to commit All dependencies properly declared renv.lock synchronized with code No missing packages detected  # Failure - requires attention Missing packages in DESCRIPTION: tidymodels, broom Package used but not in renv.lock: ggrepel Auto-fix applies the necessary additions

title: Glossary: zzcollab and Reproducible-Research Terms
text: Compendium: project folder, holding data, code, environment, write-together whole thing can re-run unit. Capture level (L0 L3): locked-project . L0 locatable (valid compendium, packages pinned), L1 pinned packages (backend present), L2 pinned environment (container Nix), L3 verified (rebuilt re-run, matching baseline). Capture feature vs validation feature: capture features (backend, Docker environment) pin environment set capture level; validation features (tests, CI, code quality) check result without changing level. ‘artifact state’: zzcollab’s design principle feature exactly primary file present, toggling feature means creating removing one file, editing scattered configuration. Five Pillars (reproducibility): five artifacts zzcollab treats jointly sufficient reproducibility: Dockerfile, renv.lock, .Rprofile, source code, research data. Feature: independently switchable capability (package backend, Docker, CI, data-integrity hashing, code-quality hooks, unit tests, cloud launch).

title: Glossary: zzcollab and Reproducible-Research Terms
text: Archetype: project type chosen zzc init (analysis, manuscript, package, simulation, blog), shapes starter layout. Profile: Docker image bundle environment built . three canonical profiles minimal (command-line, ~650MB), analysis (tidyverse, ~1.2GB), rstudio (RStudio Server, ~980MB). specialised needs (LaTeX, modeling, Shiny), extend closest profile. Distinct archetype: profile image, archetype layout. Render gate: CI check tied archetypes scaffold report, failing build manuscript render. Self-adapting generated files: generated files (Dockerfile, CI workflows) adjust whichever feature artifacts present.

title: Glossary: zzcollab and Reproducible-Research Terms
text: zzc / zzcollab: framework’s command-line interface; zzc alias zzcollab. zzc init: scaffold new compendium; asks archetype runs feature wizard (--yes skips prompts). zzc toggle / zzc add <f> / zzc rm <f>: turn features , interactively name. zzc status: read-report project’s current feature state capture level. zzc verify: confirm declared level; coherence checks artifacts agree, --full rebuilds re-runs earn L3. zzc doctor (--fix): diagnose older project back-fill .zzcollab-state record without touching primary artifacts. zzc update: minor maintenance command refreshes project’s framework-managed template files installed zzcollab version (back-fills state). .zzcollab-state: generated record (base image, digest, install mode, archetype) status verify read instead re-parsing Dockerfile. make target: named Makefile command (e.g. make docker-build, make docker-test, make check-renv) wrapping container operation.

title: Glossary: zzcollab and Reproducible-Research Terms
text: Package backend (renv / Nix / none): project records exact package versions; single mutually-exclusive choice. Auto-snapshot / auto-restore: zzcollab automatically records installed packages renv.lock container exit, installs missing packages start, replacing manual renv::snapshot() / renv::restore(). Container runtime: engine runs image: Docker, Podman (daemonless), Apptainer (HPC, single-file .sif). Team image: pre-built Docker image shared via registry every collaborator works byte-identical environment. .team-image-digest: committed file recording team image’s immutable digest members pull exact image. zzrenvcheck: zzcollab’s dependency check (run make check-renv) verifying every package used code declared DESCRIPTION pinned renv.lock. versus per-user packages added runtime (Layer 2).

previous_headings: Part II. General reproducible-research and R terms
title: Glossary: zzcollab and Reproducible-Research Terms
text: renv: R package giving project isolated, version-pinned package library. Lockfile (renv.lock): JSON record every package version needed rebuild environment. Version pinning: recording exact dependency version drift machines time. Project library: per-project package folder isolated projects. Nix / flake.nix: package manager pinned lockfile fix entire environment without container. Docker / container / image / Dockerfile: tool packages software environment; running instance, template, recipe builds . Base image: starting Docker image project builds (e.g. rocker/tidyverse, rocker/verse). Image digest pinning: referencing image immutable content hash (sha256:...) every pull byte-identical.

previous_headings: Part II. General reproducible-research and R terms
title: Glossary: zzcollab and Reproducible-Research Terms
text: Continuous integration (CI): automatically building testing code every push. CI gate / quality gate: automated pass/fail check must succeed code output accepted. Pre-commit / git hook: script runs Git event (usually commit) lint format code; R, commonly styler lintr. Unit test / testthat / tinytest: focused check one piece code behaves intended, frameworks writing checks. Code coverage: fraction code actually exercised tests. R CMD check: standard tool validating package structure, documentation, tests. Checksum / content hash: short fingerprint file’s bytes changes file changes, used data-integrity checks (e.g. data-manifest.sha256).

previous_headings: Part II. General reproducible-research and R terms
title: Glossary: zzcollab and Reproducible-Research Terms
text: Computational reproducibility: getting numerical results data code. Reproducibility crisis: widespread finding many published results independently reproduced. Code rot / environmental drift: gradual breakage working code time dependencies change underneath . Data provenance / lineage: record data came transformed. Raw vs derived data: immutable original data files versus processed, analysis-ready datasets generated . Seed (set.seed) / RNG kind: fixed starting point making random results reproducible; generator algorithm whose default changed across R versions. Research compendium: general term single version-controlled unit bundling data, code, environment, documentation (conventions rrtools).

title: Glossary: zzcollab and Reproducible-Research Terms

title: Quick Start: R Interface for ZZCOLLAB
text: guide demonstrates use ZZCOLLAB entirely R functions, without touching command line. functionality available via zzcollab shell script also accessible R package interface. Key terms vignette. New vocabulary? terms guide uses; defined first use. Full definitions vignette('glossary'). zzc / zzcollab: framework’s command-line interface, driven R-function equivalents. Profile: Docker image bundle environment built (minimal, analysis, rstudio). Package backend (renv / Nix / none): project records exact package versions; single mutually-exclusive choice. Auto-snapshot / auto-restore: zzcollab records installed packages renv.lock exit installs missing ones start. Docker / container / image: tool packages software environment; running instance template. Team image: pre-built Docker image shared via registry every collaborator works byte-identical environment. learn: Configure ZZCOLLAB R/RStudio Create reproducible projects using R functions Manage Docker environments R Work team collaboration via R interface Integrate R-based workflows Prerequisites: ZZCOLLAB installed (./install.sh) Docker installed running R RStudio (optional recommended)

title: Quick Start: R Interface for ZZCOLLAB
text: Set personal ZZCOLLAB configuration without leaving R: accomplishes: Creates ~/.zzcollab/config.yaml defaults Eliminates repetitive typing future projects Provides functionality zzcollab config commands
code: library(zzcollab)  # Initialize configuration file init_config()  # Set your defaults set_config("team_name", "myteam") set_config("github_account", "myusername") set_config("profile_name", "analysis")  # View current configuration list_config()  # Get specific values team <- get_config("team_name") profile <- get_config("profile_name")  # Validate configuration validate_config()

title: Quick Start: R Interface for ZZCOLLAB
code: # Check if a value is set team <- get_config("team_name") if (is.null(team)) {   message("Team name not configured")   set_config("team_name", "myteam") }  # Update existing configuration (built-in profiles: minimal, analysis, rstudio) set_config("profile_name", "analysis")  # View all settings config <- list_config() print(config)  # Validate before important operations if (validate_config()) {   message("Configuration is valid") } else {   stop("Configuration has errors") }

title: Quick Start: R Interface for ZZCOLLAB
text: Initialize reproducible research project entirely R: initialization, build Docker environment:
code: # Method 1: Use configuration defaults library(zzcollab)  # Create project directory dir.create("~/projects/penguin-analysis") setwd("~/projects/penguin-analysis")  # Initialize project (uses config defaults) setup_project()  # Method 2: Override specific settings setup_project(   base_image = "rocker/rstudio" ) # Build Docker image rebuild("docker-build")  # Check if build succeeded if (rebuild("docker-build")) {   message("Docker image built successfully") } else {   stop("Docker build failed") }

title: Quick Start: R Interface for ZZCOLLAB
code: # Check Docker container status container_info <- status() print(container_info)  # Add R packages to project library(zzcollab) add_package("ggplot2") add_package(c("dplyr", "tidyr", "readr"))  # Sync environment from renv.lock sync_env()  # Validate reproducibility if (validate_repro()) {   message("Environment is reproducible") } else {   warning("Reproducibility issues detected") }

title: Quick Start: R Interface for ZZCOLLAB

code: # Execute analysis script in container run_script("analysis/scripts/01_data_prep.R")  # Render R Markdown report render_report("analysis/report/report.Rmd")  # Check results if (file.exists("analysis/report/paper.html")) {   message("Report rendered successfully")   browseURL("analysis/report/paper.html") }

title: Quick Start: R Interface for ZZCOLLAB
text: Create team project R interface: initialization, build share team image: Commit push project structure:
code: library(zzcollab)  # Set team configuration. Built-in profiles are minimal, analysis, and # rstudio; for genomics, start from analysis and either add Bioconductor # packages via renv or build on a Bioconductor base image with # `zzcollab docker --base-image bioconductor/bioconductor_docker`. set_config("team_name", "genomicslab") set_config("profile_name", "analysis") set_config("github_account", "genomicslab")  # Navigate to project directory dir.create("~/projects/cancer-study") setwd("~/projects/cancer-study")  # Initialize team project init_project(   team_name = "genomicslab",   project_name = "cancer-study",   github_account = "genomicslab" ) # Build team Docker image rebuild("docker-build")  # Push to Docker Hub (requires docker login) rebuild("docker-push-team")  # Check team images images <- team_images() print(images) # Commit initial setup git_commit("Initial project setup with analysis profile")  # Push to GitHub git_push()

title: Quick Start: R Interface for ZZCOLLAB
text: Join existing project using R: Start development:
code: library(zzcollab)  # Clone repository (can use system() or do outside R) system("git clone https://github.com/genomicslab/cancer-study.git") setwd("cancer-study")  # Join project using team image join_project(   team_name = "genomicslab",   project_name = "cancer-study" )  # Verify team images available images <- team_images() print(images)  # Sync environment sync_env() # Check container status status()  # Add packages as needed add_package("tidymodels")  # Run tests rebuild("docker-test")  # Commit changes git_commit("Add tidymodels for modeling workflow") git_push()

title: Quick Start: R Interface for ZZCOLLAB
text: Get comprehensive help without leaving R: Available help topics: "general" - Main help options "init" - Team initialization "quickstart" - Quick start guide "workflow" - Daily development workflow "config" - Configuration system "renv" - Package management "docker" - Docker essentials "cicd" - CI/CD GitHub Actions "github" - GitHub integration "troubleshooting" - Common issues

title: Quick Start: R Interface for ZZCOLLAB
code: library(zzcollab)  # 1. SETUP: Configure once init_config() set_config("team_name", "myname") set_config("profile_name", "analysis")  # 2. CREATE PROJECT dir.create("~/projects/penguin-bills") setwd("~/projects/penguin-bills") setup_project()  # 3. BUILD DOCKER ENVIRONMENT if (!rebuild("docker-build")) {   stop("Docker build failed") }  # 4. DEVELOP ANALYSIS (in RStudio) # Create analysis files, write code, etc. # See vignette("quickstart") for analysis details  # 5. ADD PACKAGES (auto-captured in renv.lock on container exit) add_package("palmerpenguins") add_package("ggplot2") add_package("dplyr")  # 6. RUN TESTS rebuild("docker-test")  # 7. VALIDATE REPRODUCIBILITY if (!validate_repro()) {   stop("Reproducibility checks failed") }  # 8. VERSION CONTROL git_status() git_commit("Add bill dimensions scatter plot analysis") git_push()  # 9. CREATE PULL REQUEST create_pr(   title = "Add penguin analysis",   body = "Complete reproducible analysis with tests and CI/CD" )  # 10. CHECK CI/CD STATUS system("gh run list --limit 3")

title: Quick Start: R Interface for ZZCOLLAB
code: # View running containers containers <- status() print(containers)  # Rebuild Docker image rebuild()  # Rebuild with specific target rebuild("docker-build") rebuild("docker-test") rebuild("docker-check")  # List team images images <- team_images() if (nrow(images) > 0) {   print(images)    # Filter by team   my_images <- images[grepl("myteam", images$repository), ]   print(my_images) } else {   message("No team images found") }

title: Quick Start: R Interface for ZZCOLLAB
text: Recommended: Use Standard R Commands simplest approach use standard R commands inside containers: Alternative: R Function Interface can also use ZZCOLLAB’s R functions: Auto-Snapshot & Auto-Restore Architecture: Auto-snapshot: Packages captured renv.lock exit containers (manual renv::snapshot() needed) Auto-restore: Missing packages installed automatically start R (manual renv::restore() needed) add_package() (R function) install.packages() (standard R) work identically
code: # Inside container (make r or make docker-rstudio) install.packages("tidyverse")  # For GitHub packages: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("user/package")  # Exit container - packages automatically captured in renv.lock # Auto-restore installs them automatically next time you start R # Add single package add_package("tidyverse")  # Add multiple packages add_package(c("sf", "terra", "mapview"))  # Sync from renv.lock (e.g., after git pull) sync_env()  # Validate package consistency (runs zzrenvcheck in the container) rebuild("docker-check-renv")

title: Quick Start: R Interface for ZZCOLLAB
code: # Pre-flight checks before important operations preflight_check <- function() {   checks <- list(     config_valid = validate_config(),     repro_valid = validate_repro(),     git_clean = length(git_status()) == 0   )    all_passed <- all(unlist(checks))    if (all_passed) {     message("All pre-flight checks passed")   } else {     message("Some checks failed:")     print(checks)   }    return(all_passed) }  # Use before major operations if (preflight_check()) {   git_push()   create_pr("Add new feature", "Complete implementation with tests") }

title: Quick Start: R Interface for ZZCOLLAB
text: ZZCOLLAB provides comprehensive R interface 25+ functions: Configuration: init_config() - Initialize configuration set_config(key, value) - Set values get_config(key) - Get values list_config() - List configuration validate_config() - Validate configuration files Projects: init_project(...) - Team lead initialization join_project(...) - Team member joining setup_project(...) - Solo developer setup Docker: status() - Check containers rebuild(target) - Rebuild images team_images() - List team images Packages: add_package(packages) - Add packages sync_env() - Sync renv.lock Analysis: run_script(path) - Execute scripts render_report(path) - Render reports validate_repro() - Check reproducibility Git: git_status() - Check status git_commit(message) - Create commits git_push(branch) - Push changes create_branch(name) - Create branches create_pr(title, body) - Create PRs Help: zzcollab_help(topic) - Get help zzcollab_next_steps() - Show next steps

text: RStudio, create file R/bill_analysis.R: File → New File → R Script Save : R/bill_analysis.R
code: #' Create scatter plot of bill dimensions #' #' @param data Palmer penguins data #' @return ggplot object #' @export create_bill_plot <- function(data = palmerpenguins::penguins) {   data %>%     dplyr::filter(!is.na(bill_length_mm), !is.na(bill_depth_mm)) %>%     ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(x = bill_length_mm, y = bill_depth_mm,                                   color = species)) +     ggplot2::geom_point(size = 3, alpha = 0.7) +     ggplot2::labs(       title = 'Palmer Penguins: Bill Dimensions',       x = 'Bill Length (mm)',       y = 'Bill Depth (mm)',       color = 'Species'     ) +     ggplot2::theme_minimal() }

code: # inst/tinytest/test-bill_analysis.R # Source function from R/ source('../../R/bill_analysis.R')  # create_bill_plot produces a valid ggplot with the expected title plot <- create_bill_plot() expect_inherits(plot, 'ggplot') expect_equal(plot$labels$title, 'Palmer Penguins: Bill Dimensions')  # create_bill_plot handles missing data test_data <- palmerpenguins::penguins test_data$bill_length_mm[1:5] <- NA plot_na <- create_bill_plot(test_data) expect_inherits(plot_na, 'ggplot')  # create_bill_plot includes species in the color aesthetic expect_true('species' %in% names(plot$labels))

title: Quick Start: Complete Reproducible Data Workflow
text: quick start guide demonstrates create fully reproducible analysis 5 pillars reproducibility. create complete data workflow raw data processing, analysis, testing, reporting using Palmer Penguins bill measurements. build: Complete data workflow: raw data → processing → analysis → visualization 5 pillars: Dockerfile + renv + .Rprofile + Source Code + Research Data Functions, tests, documentation following best practices Private GitHub repository automated CI/CD validation Prerequisites: Docker installed running GitHub account (example: rgt47) Personal access token GitHub (repo permissions) zzcollab installed (./install.sh) gum installed styled TUI prompts (optional recommended): brew install gum Key terms vignette. defined first use. Full definitions vignette('glossary'). Compendium: project folder, holding data, code, write-together whole thing can re-run unit. Archetype: project type choose creation (analysis, manuscript, package, simulation, blog), shapes starter layout. Profile: Docker image bundle (R packages baked container): minimal, analysis, rstudio. Distinct archetype. Feature: independently switchable capability (package backend, Docker, CI, data hashing, code-quality hooks, unit tests, cloud launch) toggled zzc toggle zzc add / zzc rm. Capture level (L0 L3): locked-project , ‘just findable’ (L0) ‘rebuilt verified’ (L3). renv / lockfile: package backend records exact package versions renv.lock installs repeatable. Auto-snapshot: automatic renv::snapshot() runs exit R container, capturing new packages renv.lock. Five Pillars: Dockerfile, renv.lock, .Rprofile, source code, research data, necessary sufficient parts reproducible compendium.

title: Quick Start: Complete Reproducible Data Workflow
text: Create project directory run zzc init: zzc init runs identity gate first use, asks two questions. Identity gate (first run ) Fires ~/.zzcollab/config.yaml missing name email. Captures three identity fields described Step 1 saves user config. Skipped subsequent projects. Question 1 – Archetype archetype project type, shapes starter layout: analysis, manuscript, blog scaffold report (analysis/report/report.Rmd); blog also gets analysis/posts/ directory. package simulation scaffold report; simulation gets seeded, parallel-ready starter script instead. tutorial accept default, analysis. Question 2 – Feature wizard second question feature wizard (one zzc toggle uses). recommends sensible default, renv plus Docker, can accept untick. toggle independently switchable feature: package backend (renv), Docker, CI, data-integrity hashing, code-quality hooks, unit tests, cloud launch. decide everything now; features can added removed time (see vignette("quickstart3") full toggle model). tutorial, accept recommended renv plus Docker. fields differ user defaults written project’s ./zzcollab.yaml, keeping file minimal. Commit file; documents project-specific decisions allows collaborators reproduce configuration. Example ./zzcollab.yaml GitHub account differs: Non-interactive forms. --yes skips every prompt accepts defaults; --archetype <x> sets archetype flag: init, create GitHub repository: Quickstart alternative (uses config defaults, interactive prompts):
code: mkdir penguin-bills && cd penguin-bills zzc init Archetype [analysis/manuscript/package/simulation/blog] (default analysis): # Project-specific zzcollab configuration # Overrides user config (~/.zzcollab/config.yaml) # Commit this file to version control. github:   account: genomicslab # Scaffold an analysis project with default features, no prompts. zzc init --yes  # Set the archetype explicitly, still ask the feature wizard. zzc init --archetype manuscript zzc github          # creates private repo and pushes mkdir penguin-bills && cd penguin-bills zzc analysis        # init + renv + docker in one command zzc github

Available profiles (three canonical profiles): minimal - Command-line R environment (~650MB) analysis - Tidyverse data analysis (~1.2GB), recommended rstudio - RStudio Server (~980MB) specialised needs (LaTeX, modeling, Shiny), start closest one add packages inside container install.packages(), commit updated renv.lock.

title: Quick Start: Complete Reproducible Data Workflow
text: Create raw data file (simulating field-collected penguin measurements). Create analysis/data/raw_data/penguins_raw.csv: Create analysis/data/raw_data/README.md:
code: species,island,bill_length_mm,bill_depth_mm,flipper_length_mm,body_mass_g,sex,year Adelie,Torgersen,39.1,18.7,181,3750,male,2007 Adelie,Torgersen,39.5,17.4,186,3800,female,2007 Adelie,Torgersen,40.3,18.0,195,3250,female,2007 Adelie,Torgersen,NA,NA,NA,NA,NA,2007 Gentoo,Biscoe,46.1,13.2,211,4500,female,2007 Gentoo,Biscoe,50.0,16.3,230,5700,male,2007 Chinstrap,Dream,46.5,17.9,192,3500,female,2007 Chinstrap,Dream,50.0,19.5,196,3900,male,2007 # Raw Data: Palmer Penguins  ## Source Simulated penguin measurements from Palmer Station Antarctica LTER.  ## Collection Methods - Field measurements of adult penguins - Three species: Adelie, Gentoo, Chinstrap - Three islands: Torgersen, Biscoe, Dream  ## Variables - species: Penguin species (Adelie, Gentoo, Chinstrap) - island: Island where observed - bill_length_mm: Bill length in millimeters - bill_depth_mm: Bill depth in millimeters - flipper_length_mm: Flipper length in millimeters - body_mass_g: Body mass in grams - sex: Penguin sex (male, female) - year: Year of observation  ## Known Issues - Row 4 contains missing values (equipment failure) - All measurements subject to ±0.5mm measurement error

previous_headings: Step-by-Step Instructions > Step 3: Add Research Data and Processing Pipeline
title: Quick Start: Complete Reproducible Data Workflow
text: Create script clean process raw data. Create analysis/scripts/01_process_data.R: Create analysis/data/derived_data/README.md: Run processing script:
code: # Data Processing Script: Clean raw penguin data # Input: analysis/data/raw_data/penguins_raw.csv # Output: analysis/data/derived_data/penguins_clean.csv  library(dplyr)  # Load raw data raw_data <- read.csv("analysis/data/raw_data/penguins_raw.csv")  # Process data clean_data <- raw_data %>%   # Remove rows with missing values   filter(!is.na(bill_length_mm), !is.na(bill_depth_mm)) %>%   # Add derived variables   mutate(     bill_ratio = bill_length_mm / bill_depth_mm,     size_category = case_when(       body_mass_g < 3500 ~ "small",       body_mass_g < 4500 ~ "medium",       TRUE ~ "large"     )   )  # Create output directory if needed dir.create("analysis/data/derived_data", showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)  # Save processed data write.csv(clean_data, "analysis/data/derived_data/penguins_clean.csv", row.names = FALSE)  # Log processing results cat("Processed", nrow(clean_data), "valid records from", nrow(raw_data), "raw records\n") cat("Removed", nrow(raw_data) - nrow(clean_data), "incomplete records\n") # Processed Data: Palmer Penguins (Clean)  ## Source Processed from `analysis/data/raw_data/penguins_raw.csv`  ## Processing Steps See: `analysis/scripts/01_process_data.R`  1. Loaded raw data 2. Removed rows with missing bill measurements 3. Added derived variables:    - bill_ratio: bill_length_mm / bill_depth_mm    - size_category: small (<3500g), medium (3500-4500g), large (>4500g)  ## Quality Checks - All rows have complete bill measurements - All derived variables computed successfully - No outliers detected beyond expected range Rscript analysis/scripts/01_process_data.R

title: Quick Start: Complete Reproducible Data Workflow
text: Create functions R/ directory use processed data. Create R/data_utils.R: Install required packages:
code: #' Load processed penguin data #' #' @param path Path to clean data file #' @return data.frame of processed penguin data #' @export load_penguin_data <- function(path = "analysis/data/derived_data/penguins_clean.csv") {   if (!file.exists(path)) {     stop("Clean data file not found. Run analysis/scripts/01_process_data.R first.")   }    data <- read.csv(path, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)    # Validate expected columns   required_cols <- c("species", "bill_length_mm", "bill_depth_mm", "bill_ratio")   missing_cols <- setdiff(required_cols, names(data))    if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {     stop("Missing required columns: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))   }    data }  #' Calculate summary statistics by species #' #' @param data Penguin data #' @return data.frame of summary statistics #' @export summarize_by_species <- function(data) {   data %>%     dplyr::group_by(species) %>%     dplyr::summarize(       n = dplyr::n(),       mean_bill_length = mean(bill_length_mm, na.rm = TRUE),       mean_bill_depth = mean(bill_depth_mm, na.rm = TRUE),       mean_bill_ratio = mean(bill_ratio, na.rm = TRUE),       .groups = "drop"     ) } # Packages will be captured in renv.lock when you exit the container Rscript -e 'install.packages(c("dplyr", "here"))'

text: Add tests data processing functions. ZZCOLLAB projects use tinytest: test files live inst/tinytest/ contain bare top-level expectations (test_that() wrapper). Create inst/tinytest/test-data_utils.R: Run tests: accomplishes: Raw Data (Pillar 5): Original measurements analysis/data/raw_data/ Processing Pipeline: Script analysis/scripts/01_process_data.R Derived Data: Cleaned data analysis/data/derived_data/ Reusable Functions: Data utilities R/data_utils.R Unit Tests: Tests inst/tinytest/test-data_utils.R Documentation: README files documenting data provenance processing establishes complete data workflow ensures reproducibility raw data analysis.
code: # Tests for data utility functions # make docker-test loads functions from R/ via devtools::load_all()  clean_csv <- 'analysis/data/derived_data/penguins_clean.csv'  # load_penguin_data loads valid data (only when processing has been run) if (file.exists(clean_csv)) {   data <- load_penguin_data()   expect_true(is.data.frame(data))   expect_true(nrow(data) > 0)   expect_true('species' %in% names(data))   expect_true('bill_length_mm' %in% names(data))   expect_true('bill_depth_mm' %in% names(data))   expect_true('bill_ratio' %in% names(data))    # summarize_by_species produces valid output   summary <- summarize_by_species(data)   expect_true(is.data.frame(summary))   expect_true('species' %in% names(summary))   expect_true('n' %in% names(summary))   expect_true('mean_bill_length' %in% names(summary))   expect_true(is.double(summary$mean_bill_length))   expect_true(is.double(summary$mean_bill_depth))   expect_true(all(summary$mean_bill_length > 0))   expect_true(all(summary$mean_bill_depth > 0))    # bill_ratio is calculated correctly   expect_equal(data$bill_ratio,                data$bill_length_mm / data$bill_depth_mm) }  # load_penguin_data fails gracefully with a missing file expect_error(load_penguin_data('/nonexistent/path.csv'),              pattern = 'Clean data file not found') make docker-test
