Key terms vignette. New vocabulary? terms guide uses; defined first use. Full definitions vignette('glossary'). Compendium: project folder, holding data, code, environment, write-together whole thing can re-run unit. Five Pillars: five artifacts treated jointly sufficient reproducibility: Dockerfile, renv.lock, .Rprofile, source code, research data. renv / renv.lock: per-project isolated package library JSON lockfile recording every package version. Raw vs derived data: immutable original files versus processed, analysis-ready datasets generated ; cached simulation results derived_data/. title: Simulation Studies for Longitudinal Binary Data
text: Add data generation code directly manuscript develop test : Use inline R report results prose.
code:
# In simulate-inline chunk (echo=TRUE): simulate_longitudinal_binary <- function(n_subjects,                                          n_timepoints = 4,                                          beta = c(-1, 0, 0.2, 0.3),                                          sigma_b = 0.5,                                          seed = NULL) {   if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)    subject_id <- rep(1:n_subjects, each = n_timepoints)   time <- rep(0:(n_timepoints - 1), times = n_subjects)   treatment <- rep(sample(c(0, 1), n_subjects, replace = TRUE),                    each = n_timepoints)    random_intercept <- rep(rnorm(n_subjects, 0, sigma_b),                           each = n_timepoints)    linear_predictor <- beta[1] + beta[2] * treatment +                       beta[3] * time + beta[4] * treatment * time +                       random_intercept    y <- rbinom(length(linear_predictor), 1, plogis(linear_predictor))    data.frame(     subject_id = subject_id,     time = time,     treatment = factor(treatment, labels = c("control", "treatment")),     y = y   ) }  # Test it immediately test_data <- simulate_longitudinal_binary(n_subjects = 100, seed = 42) cat("Rows:", nrow(test_data), "
") cat("Subjects:", length(unique(test_data$subject_id)), "
") cat("Outcome rate:", mean(test_data$y), "
")

title: Simulation Studies for Longitudinal Binary Data
text: Add model fitting manuscript:
code:
# In fit-inline chunk (echo=TRUE): library(geepack) library(lme4)  fit_gee <- function(data, corstr = "exchangeable") {   tryCatch({     model <- geeglm(       y ~ treatment * time,       family = binomial,       data = data,       id = subject_id,       corstr = corstr     )     coefs <- summary(model)$coefficients     int_row <- grep("treatment.*:time", rownames(coefs))      list(       estimate = coefs[int_row, "Estimate"],       se = coefs[int_row, "Std.err"],       converged = TRUE     )   }, error = function(e) {     list(estimate = NA, se = NA, converged = FALSE)   }) }  fit_glmm <- function(data) {   tryCatch({     model <- glmer(       y ~ treatment * time + (1 | subject_id),       family = binomial,       data = data,       control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")     )     coefs <- summary(model)$coefficients     int_row <- grep("treatment.*:time", rownames(coefs))      list(       estimate = coefs[int_row, "Estimate"],       se = coefs[int_row, "Std. Error"],       converged = !isSingular(model)     )   }, error = function(e) {     list(estimate = NA, se = NA, converged = FALSE)   }) }  # Test on simulated data result_gee <- fit_gee(test_data) result_glmm <- fit_glmm(test_data)  cat("GEE estimate:", round(result_gee$estimate, 3), "
") cat("GLMM estimate:", round(result_glmm$estimate, 3), "
") cat("True value: 0.3
")

what: Step 4: Run Small-Scale Simulation in Manuscript
title: Simulation Studies for Longitudinal Binary Data
text: Test full workflow small simulation: Use inline R report bias values prose.
code: # In small-sim chunk (echo=TRUE): n_sims <- 50 results <- vector("list", n_sims)  for (i in 1:n_sims) {   data <- simulate_longitudinal_binary(     n_subjects = 100,     beta = c(-1, 0, 0.2, 0.3),     sigma_b = 0.5,     seed = i   )    results[[i]] <- data.frame(     sim = i,     gee_est = fit_gee(data)$estimate,     glmm_est = fit_glmm(data)$estimate   ) }  small_results <- bind_rows(results)  cat("GEE bias:", round(mean(small_results$gee_est, na.rm = TRUE) - 0.3, 3), "\n") cat("GLMM bias:", round(mean(small_results$glmm_est, na.rm = TRUE) - 0.3, 3), "\n")

title: Simulation Studies for Longitudinal Binary Data
text: code working, extract R/ files. Create R/simulate_data.R: Create R/fit_models.R: Create R/performance_metrics.R:
code: #' Generate correlated longitudinal binary data #' #' @param n_subjects Number of subjects #' @param n_timepoints Number of measurement occasions #' @param beta Fixed effects: c(intercept, treatment, time, interaction) #' @param sigma_b Random intercept SD #' @param seed Random seed #' #' @return Data frame with subject_id, time, treatment, y #' @export simulate_longitudinal_binary <- function(n_subjects,                                          n_timepoints = 4,                                          beta = c(-1, 0, 0.2, 0.3),                                          sigma_b = 0.5,                                          seed = NULL) {   if (!is.null(seed)) set.seed(seed)    if (length(beta) != 4) {     stop("beta must have 4 elements")   }    subject_id <- rep(1:n_subjects, each = n_timepoints)   time <- rep(0:(n_timepoints - 1), times = n_subjects)   treatment <- rep(sample(c(0, 1), n_subjects, replace = TRUE),                    each = n_timepoints)    random_intercept <- rep(rnorm(n_subjects, 0, sigma_b),                           each = n_timepoints)    linear_predictor <- beta[1] + beta[2] * treatment +                       beta[3] * time + beta[4] * treatment * time +                       random_intercept    y <- rbinom(length(linear_predictor), 1, plogis(linear_predictor))    data.frame(     subject_id = subject_id,     time = time,     treatment = factor(treatment, labels = c("control", "treatment")),     y = y   ) }   #' Create simulation grid #' #' @param n_subjects Vector of sample sizes #' @param beta_interaction Vector of interaction effects #' @param sigma_b Vector of random effect SDs #' @param n_sims Number of simulations per scenario #' #' @return Data frame with one row per simulation #' @export create_simulation_grid <- function(n_subjects = c(50, 100, 200),                                    beta_interaction = c(0, 0.3, 0.5),                                    sigma_b = c(0.5, 1.0),                                    n_sims = 1000) {   scenarios <- expand.grid(     n_subjects = n_subjects,     beta_interaction = beta_interaction,     sigma_b = sigma_b,     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   )   scenarios$scenario_id <- seq_len(nrow(scenarios))    sim_grid <- scenarios[rep(seq_len(nrow(scenarios)), each = n_sims), ]   sim_grid$sim_id <- rep(seq_len(n_sims), times = nrow(scenarios))   sim_grid$seed <- seq_len(nrow(sim_grid))   rownames(sim_grid) <- NULL    sim_grid } #' Fit GEE model #' #' @param data Data from simulate_longitudinal_binary #' @param corstr Correlation structure #' #' @return List with estimate, se, converged #' @export fit_gee <- function(data, corstr = "exchangeable") {   if (!requireNamespace("geepack", quietly = TRUE)) {     stop("Package 'geepack' required")   }    tryCatch({     model <- geepack::geeglm(       y ~ treatment * time,       family = binomial,       data = data,       id = subject_id,       corstr = corstr     )     coefs <- summary(model)$coefficients     int_row <- grep("treatment.*:time", rownames(coefs))      list(       estimate = coefs[int_row, "Estimate"],       se = coefs[int_row, "Std.err"],       converged = TRUE,       method = paste0("gee_", substr(corstr, 1, 4))     )   }, error = function(e) {     list(estimate = NA, se = NA, converged = FALSE,          method = paste0("gee_", substr(corstr, 1, 4)))   }) }   #' Fit GLMM #' #' @param data Data from simulate_longitudinal_binary #' #' @return List with estimate, se, converged #' @export fit_glmm <- function(data) {   if (!requireNamespace("lme4", quietly = TRUE)) {     stop("Package 'lme4' required")   }    tryCatch({     model <- lme4::glmer(       y ~ treatment * time + (1 | subject_id),       family = binomial,       data = data,       control = lme4::glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")     )     coefs <- summary(model)$coefficients     int_row <- grep("treatment.*:time", rownames(coefs))      list(       estimate = coefs[int_row, "Estimate"],       se = coefs[int_row, "Std. Error"],       converged = !lme4::isSingular(model),       method = "glmm"     )   }, error = function(e) {     list(estimate = NA, se = NA, converged = FALSE, method = "glmm")   }) }   #' Fit all models #' #' @param data Data from simulate_longitudinal_binary #' #' @return Data frame with results from all methods #' @export fit_all_models <- function(data) {   results <- list(     gee_exch = fit_gee(data, "exchangeable"),     gee_ar1 = fit_gee(data, "ar1"),     gee_ind = fit_gee(data, "independence"),     glmm = fit_glmm(data)   )    do.call(rbind, lapply(names(results), function(name) {     r <- results[[name]]     data.frame(       method = name,       estimate = r$estimate,       se = r$se,       converged = r$converged     )   })) } #' Calculate performance metrics #' #' @param estimates Vector of estimates #' @param ses Vector of standard errors #' @param true_value True parameter value #' @param alpha Significance level #' #' @return Named vector of metrics #' @export calculate_performance <- function(estimates, ses, true_value, alpha = 0.05) {   valid <- !is.na(estimates) & !is.na(ses)   estimates <- estimates[valid]   ses <- ses[valid]    if (length(estimates) < 10) {     return(c(n_valid = length(estimates), bias = NA, coverage = NA, power = NA))   }    z <- qnorm(1 - alpha / 2)    bias <- mean(estimates) - true_value   empirical_se <- sd(estimates)   avg_se <- mean(ses)   se_ratio <- avg_se / empirical_se    lower <- estimates - z * ses   upper <- estimates + z * ses   coverage <- mean(lower <= true_value & upper >= true_value)    power <- mean(abs(estimates / ses) > z)    c(     n_valid = length(estimates),     bias = bias,     empirical_se = empirical_se,     avg_se = avg_se,     se_ratio = se_ratio,     coverage = coverage,     power = power   ) }

title: Simulation Studies for Longitudinal Binary Data
text: project uses tinytest. Create inst/tinytest/test_simulate_data.R bare top-level expectations (test_that() wrapper):
code: # simulate_longitudinal_binary returns correct structure data <- simulate_longitudinal_binary(n_subjects = 10, seed = 42) expect_inherits(data, 'data.frame') expect_equal(nrow(data), 40) expect_true(all(c('subject_id', 'time', 'treatment', 'y') %in% names(data)))  # simulate_longitudinal_binary respects seed data1 <- simulate_longitudinal_binary(n_subjects = 20, seed = 123) data2 <- simulate_longitudinal_binary(n_subjects = 20, seed = 123) expect_identical(data1, data2)  # simulate_longitudinal_binary produces valid outcomes data_valid <- simulate_longitudinal_binary(n_subjects = 100, seed = 42) expect_true(all(data_valid$y %in% c(0, 1)))  # create_simulation_grid produces correct dimensions grid <- create_simulation_grid(   n_subjects = c(50, 100),   beta_interaction = c(0, 0.3),   sigma_b = 0.5,   n_sims = 10 ) expect_equal(nrow(grid), 2 * 2 * 1 * 10)

what: Step 7: Move Full Simulation to Scripts
title: Simulation Studies for Longitudinal Binary Data
text: Create analysis/scripts/02_run_simulation.R:
code: # 02_run_simulation.R # Run full simulation - execute separately from manuscript # Usage: Rscript analysis/scripts/02_run_simulation.R  library(furrr) library(progressr)  source("R/simulate_data.R") source("R/fit_models.R")  plan(multisession, workers = parallel::detectCores() - 1)  sim_grid <- create_simulation_grid(   n_subjects = c(50, 100, 200),   beta_interaction = c(0, 0.3, 0.5),   sigma_b = c(0.5, 1.0),   n_sims = 1000 )  cat("Running", nrow(sim_grid), "simulations\n")  run_one <- function(row) {   data <- simulate_longitudinal_binary(     n_subjects = row$n_subjects,     beta = c(-1, 0, 0.2, row$beta_interaction),     sigma_b = row$sigma_b,     seed = row$seed   )    results <- fit_all_models(data)   results$scenario_id <- row$scenario_id   results$sim_id <- row$sim_id   results }  handlers(global = TRUE)  with_progress({   p <- progressor(nrow(sim_grid))   results_list <- future_map(     seq_len(nrow(sim_grid)),     function(i) { p(); run_one(sim_grid[i, ]) },     .options = furrr_options(seed = TRUE)   ) })  results <- bind_rows(results_list) saveRDS(results, "analysis/data/derived_data/simulation_results.rds") cat("Saved to analysis/data/derived_data/simulation_results.rds\n")

title: Simulation Studies for Longitudinal Binary Data
text: Update report.Rmd use cached results:
code: # In load-results chunk: source("../../R/performance_metrics.R") results <- readRDS("../data/derived_data/simulation_results.rds")  scenarios <- results |>   select(scenario_id) |>   distinct() |>   left_join(     create_simulation_grid(n_sims = 1) |>       select(scenario_id, n_subjects, beta_interaction, sigma_b) |>       distinct()   )  # In compute-performance chunk: performance <- results |>   left_join(scenarios) |>   group_by(scenario_id, n_subjects, beta_interaction, sigma_b, method) |>   summarise(     perf = list(calculate_performance(estimate, se, first(beta_interaction))),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   unnest_wider(perf)  # In table-bias chunk: performance |>   filter(beta_interaction == 0.3) |>   select(n_subjects, sigma_b, method, bias) |>   pivot_wider(names_from = method, values_from = bias) |>   knitr::kable(digits = 3, caption = "Bias by sample size and method")  # In fig-power chunk: performance |>   filter(beta_interaction != 0) |>   mutate(effect = paste("OR =", round(exp(beta_interaction), 2))) |>   ggplot(aes(n_subjects, power, color = method, linetype = factor(sigma_b))) +   geom_hline(yintercept = 0.8, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray") +   geom_line() +   geom_point() +   facet_wrap(~effect) +   scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0, 1), labels = scales::percent) +   labs(x = "Sample Size", y = "Power", color = "Method", linetype = "sigma") +   theme_minimal()

title: Plot Viewing with zzvim-R Terminal Graphics
text: zzvim-R vim plugin provides automatic plot display kitty iTerm2 terminals. Unlike manual save--view approaches, zzvim-R intercepts R’s graphics device displays plots automatically created. Key terms vignette. New vocabulary? terms guide uses; defined first use. Full definitions vignette('glossary'). Docker / container: tool packages software environment, running instance environment. .Rprofile: R session-configuration file zzcollab generates, sources local override . .Rprofile.local: per-machine, gitignored override file zzvim-R writes terminal graphics functions. make target: named Makefile command (e.g. make r) wrapping container operation. DESCRIPTION: package-metadata file marks directory R project root. vignette covers: Installation setup Auto-display functions (zzplot, zzggplot) Plot history navigation Configuration options (size, alignment, relative sizing) Split pane viewing Integration zzcollab Docker workflows

code: ✓ Terminal graphics enabled (KITTY)  Quick reference:   zzplot(...)                : Base R plots with auto-display   zzggplot(p)                : ggplot2 plots with auto-display   plot_history()             : View plot history   plot_prev() / plot_next()  : Navigate history   save_plot(file)            : Save current plot as PNG   plot_to_pdf(file)          : Save current plot as PDF   set_plot_size(w,h,dpi)     : Fixed dimensions   set_plot_size_relative()   : Size by % of terminal (default: 75% x 100%)   set_plot_align(a)          : Alignment (left/center/right)   plot_split()               : Open current plot in split pane   plot_redisplay_if_resized(): Redisplay after terminal resize

title: Authors and Citation
text: Ronald G. Thomas. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Thomas R (2026). zzcollab: Docker-based Research Collaboration Framework. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/rgt47/zzcollab.
code: @Manual{,   title = {zzcollab: Docker-based Research Collaboration Framework},   author = {Ronald G. Thomas},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/rgt47/zzcollab}, }

title: zzcollab
text: systematic framework reproducible research collaboration using Docker containers. zzcollab provides command-line tools R interfaces create, manage, collaborate research projects standardized Docker environments, automated CI/CD workflows, team collaboration tools.

title: zzcollab
text: Single structure supporting entire research lifecycle (data analysis paper package) Marwick/rrtools compatible directory layout Comprehensive tutorial library (framework repository, installed projects) Docker-based environments reproducible research Team collaboration shared base images R package interface integration R workflows Advanced configuration system user/project-level settings Three Docker profiles: minimal, analysis, rstudio Profile-based architecture: Team lead selects Docker profile, members add packages needed Automated CI/CD workflows Analysis reporting tools Git integration version control Command-line tools automation Comprehensive documentation examples

title: zzcollab
code: # Install zzcollab command-line tool git clone https://github.com/rgt47/zzcollab.git cd zzcollab ./install.sh

title: zzcollab
code: # install.packages('pak') pak::pak('rgt47/zzcollab')  library(zzcollab)

title: zzcollab
text: zzcollab follows research compendium framework proposed Marwick, Boettiger, Mullen (2018), providing standardized structure reproducible research projects.

title: zzcollab
code: project/ ├── analysis/ │   ├── data/ │   │   ├── raw_data/         # Original, unmodified data │   │   └── derived_data/     # Processed, analysis-ready data │   ├── report/ │   │   ├── report.Rmd        # Manuscript │   │   └── references.bib │   ├── figures/              # Generated visualizations │   └── scripts/              # Analysis code ├── R/                        # Reusable functions (add as needed) ├── tests/                    # Unit tests (add as needed) ├── Dockerfile                # Computational environment └── renv.lock                 # Package versions

title: zzcollab
text: research workflows supported: Data Analysis: Use analysis/scripts/ figures/ Manuscript Writing: Use analysis/report/report.Rmd Package Development: Use R/, man/, tests/ Complete Compendium: Use directories full reproducibility Progressive disclosure: Start data analysis, add manuscript writing, extract functions R/ reusing code. migration required research evolves.

title: zzcollab
text: Tutorial examples complete projects available : https://github.com/rgt47/zzcollab/tree/main/examples Step--step tutorials EDA, modeling, validation Complete example research compendia Reusable code patterns

title: zzcollab
text: zzcollab implements two-layer reproducibility architecture:

title: zzcollab
text: team lead selects Docker profile defines foundational environment: Base R version (e.g., R 4.4.0) System dependencies (GDAL, PROJ, LaTeX, etc.) Pre-installed packages (tidyverse, sf, etc.) Three profiles available: minimal, analysis, rstudio Key principle: selected, Docker profile fixed team. Team members change base image ensure consistent environments.

title: zzcollab
text: team members can independently add R packages needed: exit container, packages automatically captured renv.lock. lock file accumulates packages team members (union model), ensuring everyone access required packages maintaining flexibility. GitHub packages: Alternative: renv::install() works CRAN GitHub packages:
code: # Inside the Docker container install.packages("tidymodels") install.packages("plotly") install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("user/package") renv::install("tidymodels")              # CRAN renv::install("user/package")            # GitHub

title: zzcollab
text: Team Lead (one-time setup): Team Member (joining project):
code: zzcollab config set dockerhub-account mylab   # one-time mkdir study && cd study zzcollab tidyverse                             # init + renv + docker (tidyverse) zzcollab dockerhub                            # push team image to Docker Hub git add . && git commit -m "Initial setup" && git push git clone https://github.com/mylab/study.git && cd study make docker-build             # Build Docker image from Dockerfile make r # Inside container: install.packages("package") as needed

title: zzcollab
code: library(zzcollab)  # Set up your defaults once set_config("team_name", "myteam") set_config("github_account", "myusername")  # View your configuration list_config()

title: zzcollab
code: # Using config defaults (recommended) init_project(project_name = "myproject")  # Or with explicit parameters init_project(   team_name = "myteam",   project_name = "myproject" )

title: zzcollab
code: # Using config defaults join_project(project_name = "myproject")  # Or with explicit parameters join_project(   team_name = "myteam",   project_name = "myproject" )

title: zzcollab
text: zzcollab provides several Docker profiles optimized different research needs. profile selected creating project (switched later existing project). specialised needs (LaTeX, Shiny, machine learning), start one profiles add packages inside container install.packages(), commit updated renv.lock. Run zzcollab list full set available profiles bundles.

title: zzcollab
code: # Select the profile when creating the project (run inside the project directory) zzcollab tidyverse

title: zzcollab
text: Docker profile set, team members add packages needed: Packages automatically captured renv.lock exit container - manual snapshot needed!
code: # Enter container (starts R directly) make r  # Inside R - add packages using standard R > install.packages("tidymodels") > install.packages("here") > q()  # Exit R → returns to host (auto-snapshot runs on exit)  # renv.lock automatically updated and validated!  # Commit the updated renv.lock git add renv.lock git commit -m "Add tidymodels and here packages" git push

title: zzcollab
text: zzcollab includes hierarchical configuration system establish project defaults reduce parameter specification.

title: zzcollab
text: User config: ~/.zzcollab/config.yaml (personal defaults) Project config: ./zzcollab.yaml (project-specific overrides) Priority: project > user > built-defaults

title: zzcollab
code: zzcollab config init                    # Create default config file zzcollab config set team-name "myteam"  # Set configuration value zzcollab config get team-name           # Get configuration value zzcollab config list                    # List all configuration zzcollab config validate               # Validate YAML syntax

title: zzcollab
text: Team settings: team_name, github_account Forge: forge (github | gitlab | none), gitlab_account, gitlab_host (self-hosted) Automation: auto_github, skip_confirmation

title: zzcollab
text: get_config() - Get configuration values set_config() - Set configuration values list_config() - List configuration validate_config() - Validate configuration files init_config() - Initialize default config

title: zzcollab
text: init_project() - Initialize team project (config-aware) join_project() - Join existing project (config-aware) setup_project() - Setup individual project (config-aware)

text: status() - Check container status rebuild() - Rebuild Docker images team_images() - List team images

title: zzcollab
text: add_package() - Add R packages sync_env() - Sync environment renv

title: zzcollab
text: run_script() - Execute R scripts container render_report() - Render analysis reports validate_repro() - Check reproducibility

title: zzcollab
text: git_status() - Check git status git_commit() - Create commits git_push() - Push GitHub create_pr() - Create pull requests create_branch() - Create feature branches

title: zzcollab
code: # 0. One-time setup (configure your defaults) set_config("team_name", "datascience")  # 1. Initialize project (uses config defaults) init_project(project_name = "covid-analysis")  # 2. Add required packages (inside container via make r) # In container: #   install.packages(c("tidyverse", "lubridate", "plotly")) # Packages automatically captured on exit  # 3. Create feature branch create_branch("feature/exploratory-analysis")  # 4. Run analysis run_script("analysis/scripts/exploratory_analysis.R")  # 5. Render report render_report("analysis/report/report.Rmd")  # 6. Validate reproducibility validate_repro()  # 7. Commit and push git_commit("Add COVID-19 exploratory analysis") git_push()

title: zzcollab
code: your-project/ ├── analysis/              # Research workspace │   ├── data/ │   │   ├── raw_data/     # Original, unmodified data │   │   └── derived_data/ # Processed data │   ├── report/ │   │   ├── report.Rmd    # Manuscript │   │   └── references.bib │   ├── figures/          # Generated visualizations │   └── scripts/          # Analysis code ├── R/                    # Reusable functions ├── tests/                # Unit tests ├── .github/workflows/    # CI/CD automation ├── DESCRIPTION           # Project metadata ├── Dockerfile            # Computational environment ├── Makefile              # Automation commands └── README.md

title: zzcollab
code: zzcollab <command> [options] zzcollab <profile>            # quickstart: init + renv + docker zzcollab config <subcommand>  COMMANDS (can be combined):   init, renv, docker, git, github, gitlab, push (alias: dockerhub)   build, doctor, validate, config, list, help  PROFILES (quickstart, or switch profile in an existing project):   minimal, analysis, rstudio  GLOBAL OPTIONS:   -v, --verbose            More output   -q, --quiet              Errors only   -y, --yes  /  -Y         Accept defaults (non-interactive)   --no-build               Skip Docker build prompt   --version                Print version   -h, --help               Show help message  PER-COMMAND OPTIONS:   init:      --force                  Scaffold even if the directory is not empty   docker:    -b, --build              Build image after generating              -r, --profile NAME       Select profile (tidyverse, minimal, ...)              --base-image IMG         Override base image (default: rocker/r-ver)              --r-version VER          Pin R version   push:      -t, --tag TAG            Image tag (default: latest); alias: dockerhub   github:    --private | --public     Repo visibility (default: private)   gitlab:    --private | --public | --internal   Repo visibility (default: private)   rm:        -f, --force              Skip the removal confirmation prompt  CONFIG COMMANDS:   zzcollab config init                    # Create default config   zzcollab config set KEY VALUE           # Set configuration value   zzcollab config get KEY                 # Get configuration value   zzcollab config list                    # List all configuration   zzcollab config validate                # Validate YAML syntax  EXAMPLES:   # Configuration (one-time)   zzcollab config set dockerhub-account myteam   # Docker Hub namespace   zzcollab config set github-account myorg       # GitHub namespace   # GitLab instead of GitHub (single forge):   zzcollab config set forge gitlab               # use GitLab   zzcollab config set gitlab-account mylab       # GitLab namespace    # Solo researcher   mkdir study && cd study   zzcollab tidyverse                           # Full setup (tidyverse)    # Team collaboration - Lead   cd study && zzcollab tidyverse   zzcollab push                               # Push team image to the registry   zzcollab github                             # Create repo + push   # (with forge=gitlab: zzcollab gitlab)    # Team collaboration - Member   git clone https://github.com/myteam/study.git && cd study   make docker-build                           # Build from project Dockerfile   make r                                      # Start development   # Inside container: install.packages("pkg") as needed    # Custom base image   zzcollab docker --base-image rgt47/r-pluspackages

title: Get configuration value from zzcollab configuration system — get_config
text: Character string configuration value, NULL key set configuration file. Returns highest priority value key exists multiple configuration files.

title: Get configuration value from zzcollab configuration system — get_config
text: function interfaces zzcollab shell script's configuration system, manages YAML configuration files across project, user, system levels. provides consistent configuration management R shell interfaces. Configuration precedence (highest lowest): Project-level: ./zzcollab.yaml (project-specific overrides) User-level: ~/.zzcollab/config.yaml (personal defaults) System-level: /etc/zzcollab/config.yaml (system-wide defaults)

title: Get configuration value from zzcollab configuration system — get_config

")  # Get GitHub account with fallback github <- get_config("github_account") %||% get_config("team_name") } # }

title: Get configuration value with fallback default — get_config_default
text: internal function implements "null-coalescing" pattern commonly used throughout zzcollab configuration management. provides clean way specify fallback values configuration keys might set. function particularly useful zzcollab functions need handle optional configuration parameters gracefully. eliminates need repeated NULL checking provides consistent behavior across codebase. Usage Pattern: function typically used internally zzcollab functions provide sensible defaults users configured specific values.

title: Get configuration value with fallback default — get_config_default

title: Initialize a new zzcollab team project — init_project
text: team_name Character string specifying Docker Hub team/organization name. becomes part Docker image names (e.g., "myteam/projectcore"). NULL, uses value configuration file via get_config("team_name"). project_name Character string specifying project name. compendium created current working directory (named accordingly); value validated used status messages. Must valid Docker repository name (lowercase, spaces). github_account Character string specifying GitHub account repository creation. NULL, uses config default falls back team_name. Used GitHub CLI create private repositories. profile Character string naming Docker profile / quickstart bundle scaffold (e.g. "analysis", "minimal", "rstudio"). Defaults "analysis".

title: Initialize a new zzcollab team project — init_project
text: Logical value indicating success (TRUE) failure (FALSE) scaffolding step.

title: Initialize a new zzcollab team project — init_project
text: function performs two steps using current zzcollab CLI: Configuration: Records DockerHub account (GitHub account, given) via zzcollab config set later dockerhub github commands publish correct accounts. Scaffolding: Runs profile quickstart (zzcollab <profile>), creates R package structure, renv.lock, Dockerfile. publish team image repository afterwards, run zzcollab dockerhub zzcollab github project directory. Prerequisites: Docker installed running Docker Hub account image hosting GitHub CLI authenticated (repository creation) zzcollab installed PATH source directory Team Workflow: Team Lead: Runs init_project() per project Team Members: Use join_project() join existing projects

title: Initialize a new zzcollab team project — init_project
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic team project initialization success <- init_project(   team_name = "mylab",   project_name = "covid-study" )  # Full specification with all parameters success <- init_project(   team_name = "datascience",   project_name = "market-analysis",   github_account = "myuniversity" )  # Using configuration defaults (recommended workflow) # First, set up your defaults set_config("team_name", "mylab")  # Then initialize projects easily init_project(project_name = "new-study") } # }

Clone repository (outside R) # system("git clone https://github.com/mylab/study.git") # setwd("study")  # 2. Join project join_project(team_name = "mylab", project_name = "study")  # 3. title: Validate zzcollab configuration files — validate_config
text: Logical: TRUE every existing configuration file syntactically valid YAML, FALSE fails parse.

title: Validate zzcollab configuration files — validate_config
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Validate configuration before important operations if (validate_config()) {   cat("Configuration is valid, proceeding...\n")   init_project(project_name = "my-study") } else {   cat("Configuration has errors, please fix before proceeding\n")   list_config()  # Show current config for debugging }  # Use in automated workflows validate_config() || stop("Invalid zzcollab configuration")  # Validation after making changes set_config("profile_name", "rstudio") if (!validate_config()) {   warning("Configuration may have issues") } } # }

what: zzcollab: Docker-based Research Collaboration Framework — zzcollab-package
title: zzcollab: Docker-based Research Collaboration Framework — zzcollab-package
text: comprehensive framework reproducible research collaboration using Docker containers. Provides R interfaces create, manage, collaborate research projects standardized Docker environments, automated CI/CD workflows, team collaboration tools.

title: zzcollab: Docker-based Research Collaboration Framework — zzcollab-package
text: Maintainer: Ronald G. Thomas rgthomas@ucsd.edu (ORCID)

title: Get zzcollab help — zzcollab_help
code: zzcollab_help(topic = NULL)

title: Get zzcollab help — zzcollab_help
text: topic Character string specifying help page display. Options include: NULL "general": Main help command-line options (default) "docker": Docker essentials researchers "profiles": Available Docker profiles "config": Configuration system guide "next-steps": Development workflow guidance

title: Get zzcollab help — zzcollab_help
text: Character vector help text, invisible NULL displayed via pager. help text formatted ANSI colors terminal display.

title: Get zzcollab help — zzcollab_help
text: function provides access zzcollab's comprehensive help system directly R. help page designed accessible researchers without extensive DevOps knowledge, focusing practical workflows examples. help pages displayed using system's pager (usually 'less') running interactively, allowing easy navigation longer help content.

title: Get zzcollab help — zzcollab_help
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Display main help zzcollab_help()  # Learn about configuration system zzcollab_help("config")  # Available Docker profiles zzcollab_help("profiles")  # Docker basics for researchers zzcollab_help("docker") } # }
